intro: Guapira graciliflora, also known as Guapira or Brazilian cherry, is a shrub-like plant native to the tropical forests of South America, belonging to the family Polygonaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Guapira graciliflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
